summary: Documentation diagram omits 200ms debounce sub-window, misleading developers about escalation behavior
failure_scenario: Developer reads diagram showing 'CancelRequested -> Second Ctrl+C within 1s -> ExitRequested' and writes test expecting exit after 50ms double-tap. Test fails because debounce returns Cancel, not Exit. Developer wastes hours debugging 'wrong' logic.

summary: Comment says 'spawn_blocking' but code uses 'tokio::spawn'; redundant write to already-Disabled status; dropped JoinHandle
failure_scenario: Developer reads comment, assumes spawn_blocking is used, adds blocking I/O inside spawned task thinking it runs on blocking pool. Task actually runs on async runtime, blocking tokio worker thread - exactly the problem the fix was trying to avoid.

summary: render_tree_detail() duplicates identical pattern from tool_summary.rs lines 249-256
failure_scenario: Tree prefix style changes (e.g., '└' to Unicode box-drawing). Two locations must be updated in lockstep. Mismatch produces visually inconsistent output between file-operation and tool-call summaries.

summary: notify_one() may delay cancellation in batch tool execution - only one of N+1 concurrent waiters is woken
failure_scenario: User starts 5 parallel tools, presses Ctrl+C. notify_one wakes tool future #3. Tools #1, #2, #4, #5 continue executing until they complete or timeout. User sees delayed/partial cancellation instead of immediate stop.

summary: Thread-safety test assertion is too weak - passes trivially even if no concurrency occurs
failure_scenario: Test asserts 'is_cancel_requested() || is_exit_requested()' which is true after first register_signal() call. Test spawns 10 threads x 100 iterations but assertion doesn't verify atomicity or consistency under contention.

summary: Stale '// Pagination / read plumbing' comment remains after entries were removed
failure_scenario: Developer sees comment header with no entries beneath it, confused about whether removal was intentional or merge artifact.
